title: Fiery Peruvian Cheese Tacu Tacu
description: A lacto-vegetarian friendly Peruvian dish featuring fennel seeds and kombu cold extract, with a charred and cheesy texture, perfect for a quick fix meal. This tacu tacu recipe combines the flavors of Peru with a spicy kick.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the fennel seeds and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the cooked rice, canned beans, and kombu cold extract, and stir to combine.
Add the shredded cheese and stir until melted and well combined.
Add the diced aji amarillo pepper and stir to combine.
Using a spatula, shape the mixture into a rectangular shape, about 1 inch thick.
Cook for an additional 5 minutes, until the bottom is charred and the cheese is melted and pulled.
Flip the tacu tacu and cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the other side is also charred.
